proposals.reason: ISO/TS 24971-2 now in force (July 2026); introduces structured ML/AI risk assessment framework mandatory for SaMD compliance reports before notified body submission.

card.t: ISO/TS 24971-2 — ML/AI Risk Management in Force
card.x: Structured guidance on training data quality, algorithmic drift, model interpretability, and robustness validation. MDR compliance dossiers must demonstrate alignment to avoid notified body RFIs.

proposals.reason: MDCG 2026-4 guidance published (June 2026); defines SSCP roles, responsibilities, and EUDAMED registration procedures with Q3 2026 compliance deadline for new clinical trials.

card.x: Consolidated guidance on sponsor and investigator SSCP validation procedures. Conformity required by end Q3 2026 for any new MDR clinical trial launch.

proposals.reason: CNIL methodologies MR-001 and MR-003 applied since May 2026; require authentication, access control, audit trails, encryption, and incident response. Health SaMD and cloud infrastructure must align before audit.

card.t: CNIL MR-001 & MR-003 — Health Data Security Modernization
card.x: Authentication, authorization, audit logging (MR-001) and encryption, network segmentation, incident management (MR-003) now mandatory. e-health projects must audit HDS/C5 alignment immediately.

proposals.reason: Swissdamed mandatory since 1 July 2026; replaces paper registry with IT-based system. Retroactive submissions accepted until October 2026 for already-commercialized devices.

card.x: All devices marketed in Switzerland must be registered in Swissdamed before launch. Includes manufacturer, distributor, classification, and conformity documentation. Retroactive catch-up until October 2026.

proposals.reason: MHRA AI Airlock phase 2 report published 9 June 2026; establishes UK AI qualification model (training, adversarial robustness, transparency, audit). Offers insight into agile AI assessment pathway alternative to rigid EU MDR approach.

card.t: MHRA AI Airlock Phase 2 — AI Evaluation Framework Published
card.x: Details qualification process: training data validation, adversarial robustness, model transparency, independent audit. Sandbox available. Multi-market strategy (UK pilot then EU) now viable for SaMD.

proposals.reason: MDR articles 52.4 and 61.6 revisions underway to clarify exemptions for passive implants. No publication date confirmed; manufacturers should anticipate changes to conformity file scope.

card.t: MDR Articles 52.4 & 61.6 — Implant Exemptions Under Review
card.x: Clarification in progress on exemptions for passive implants (e.g. simple vascular clips). Dossiers in evaluation should verify if product qualifies under revised criteria. Monitor Commission calendar.
